NOTE — distinct from UC #4's Vendor Composite Performance Score: RISK SCORE answers 'is this vendor financially/legally safe to transact with' (GST + MCA + credit + litigation); PERFORMANCE SCORE answers 'should we recommend this vendor for this category' (price + delivery + quality + risk-tier penalty). The two are linked: Risk Score feeds Performance Score as a penalty input, but they are not the same score.

description: Multi-criteria weighted composite ranking vendors for recommendation fitness per category: price competitiveness + on-time delivery rate + quality rejection rate + risk-tier penalty; drives the AI-ranked vendor list surfaced to sourcing managers
notesOpenQuestions:
DISTINCT FROM UC #5's COMPOSITE VENDOR RISK SCORE — commonly confused. PERFORMANCE SCORE answers 'should we recommend this vendor for this category' and weights price + delivery + quality with a risk-tier penalty. RISK SCORE answers 'is this vendor financially/legally safe to transact with' and weights GST + MCA + credit + litigation. The two are linked: Risk Score feeds Performance Score as a penalty input.

Component weightings may need to be category-specific (CP weights quality higher, packaging weights price). Define in workshops with Category Managers + Sourcing leadership.
relatedUseCases: Procurement #4 - AI-Led Vendor Identification & Recommendation
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Define weighting framework with category managers (likely category-specific); build scoring pipeline aggregating Per-Vendor Pricing Pattern (UC #3) + GRN-derived delivery/quality metrics + UC #5 Risk Tier penalty; refresh weekly; surface as the primary ranking signal in the recommendation interface.

description: Audit-trail event record fired when a buyer clicks 'RFQ this vendor' from the recommendation interface; captures recommendation-vendor pair, buyer ID, commodity, timestamp, and downstream RFQ outcome — closing the loop between recommendation and actual sourcing event
notesOpenQuestions: Creates a recommendation → RFQ → outcome learning loop analogous to UC #3's Negotiation Outcome Log. Without it, the recommendation engine cannot learn from buyer acceptance/rejection patterns. Capture: triggered automatically when the recommendation interface fires the Ariba RFQ; outcome (awarded vendor, final price) backfilled from Ariba once RFQ closes.

whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Build outbound integration from recommendation interface → Ariba RFQ API; store trigger event with recommendation context (which vendors were ranked, which was chosen); webhook from Ariba on RFQ close to backfill awarded vendor and final price; surface in dashboard as recommendation→outcome conversion KPI.

description: Raw voice + transcript artifact captured by TBMs in Saathi App after dealer visits; primary capture mode is Voice Note (free-form, unstructured) to preserve colloquial nuance, vernacular phrasing, and compositional intelligence that a Voice AI Agent's question-answer structure would flatten; Voice AI Agent surface available as optional secondary mode for specific structured workflows (e.g., new-TBM onboarding, scheme-objection capture). Raw audio is the primary persisted artifact; transcript is derived.
notesOpenQuestions:
DESIGN DECISION: Raw voice note is the PRIMARY capture surface. Voice AI Agent is secondary, not primary — the colloquial nuance, code-switched Hindi-English-local-language, and compositional intelligence in raw speech is itself a strategic moat (vernacular agri-sales voice corpus). An agent's question-answer scaffolding flattens this.

ON-GROUND TESTING REQUIRED before full rollout: pilot in 2–3 regions (Hindi-heavy, South Indian, mixed code-switched) over 2 months to validate (a) transcription quality across actual TBM languages and dialects; (b) field noise tolerance (markets, dealer shops, motorbikes); (c) domain vocabulary accuracy (seed variety names, agrochemical molecules, scheme names) — expect 15–25% errors without a glossary layer; (d) TBM adoption and trust dynamics; (e) privacy comfort levels.

FEASIBILITY ASSESSMENT: technically buildable today — 6–8 weeks to functional MVP, 3–4 months to production quality. STT options: AI4Bharat IndicWhisper / IndicConformer (open source, Indian-language specialist), Sarvam AI, Krutrim, Whisper-large-v3, Azure / Google STT. Self-hosting likely cheaper at SFS scale than commercial API pricing ($5–15K/month at scale).

RETENTION: raw audio should be persisted indefinitely. STT model quality is improving rapidly (3 generations of Indic models from AI4Bharat in 24 months); re-transcription in 12–18 months will be materially better.

PLATFORM CAPABILITY: the in-app voice capture infrastructure is reusable across UC #11 (Sales Coaching), UC #12 (Product & Partner Visibility), UC #14 (Churn Early Warning). Design as a Saathi platform feature.

Phase 2 (Production, 3–4 months): domain glossary layer (seed varieties, agrochemicals, scheme names); structured extraction LLM layer; dealer-profile display surface; feedback loop for transcription corrections. Phase 3 (Platform): expose as reusable Saathi capability for downstream UCs (#11, #12, #14).

whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Audit current geo-location coverage; back-capture missing coordinates via Saathi check-in or TBM-confirmed updates; governance for re-validation on dealer relocation.

description: Dealer's credit terms with SFS: credit limit, payment terms (days), current outstanding balance, ageing buckets; basis for credit-risk dimension of dealer tier algorithm
relatedUseCases: Field Force #4 - Dealer Profiles & Field-Visit Conversation Inputs (Saathi app)
Field Force #6 - Unified Product & Partner-Level Visibility
Field Force #8 - Early Warning for Sales Performance & Dealer Churn Risk
Enterprise Assistant #1 - Enterprise Chat Assistant (Supply & Sales)

description: Number of dealer visits per TBM per period (week / month / quarter); the basic activity-volume metric for the dashboard
notesOpenQuestions: Pair with outcome metrics (sales, scheme participation) when surfacing — visit count in isolation incentivizes gaming. See use case Open Questions for the activity-outcome correlation design tension.
relatedUseCases: Field Force #2 - Activity Analytics (Growthbook + SAP)
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Aggregate TBM Visit Log records per period; refresh daily; surface alongside outcome metrics.

description: Decomposition of TBM time by activity type, dealer tier, BU, and call type; surfaces patterns like 'TBM X spends 70% of time on Bronze dealers' or 'TBM Y spends 50% on complaint calls'; basis for coaching interventions
notesOpenQuestions: Time derived from visit duration (Saathi check-in/out timestamps) plus voice-capture duration. Time spent in transit not captured — may need addition if material to coaching.
relatedUseCases: Field Force #2 - Activity Analytics (Growthbook + SAP)
Field Force #5 - AI-Enabled Sales Coaching
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Aggregate visit duration + call type + dealer tier + BU per TBM per period; surface as stacked-bar visualization in dashboard; benchmark against region / BU peers.

description: % of assigned dealers visited within tier-appropriate cadence; e.g., Platinum dealers expected every 15 days, Gold every 30, Silver every 45, Bronze every 60; surfaces 'X Gold-tier dealers haven't been visited in 45+ days' alerts
notesOpenQuestions: Tier-specific cadence thresholds defined with Sales leadership. Reuses Dealer Tier (UC #10) + Last Visit Timestamp (UC #10). Coverage gap alerts route to TBM + RBM for action.
relatedUseCases: Field Force #2 - Activity Analytics (Growthbook + SAP)
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Define tier-specific cadence thresholds with Sales leadership; compute (dealers visited within window) ÷ (dealers assigned) per tier per TBM; alerting engine for gap dealers; route to TBM dashboard + RBM coaching view.

description: Correlation analysis between activity patterns (visit cadence, call type mix, time allocation) and sales outcomes (primary sales, secondary sales, scheme participation, dealer tier movement); identifies high-leverage activity patterns from top-performing TBMs
notesOpenQuestions:
DESIGN TENSION FLAGGED: Done well, this surfaces best-practice patterns that propagate across the field force (high-performing TBMs visit Platinum dealers more frequently, spend more time on discovery calls, etc.). Done badly, this becomes surveillance theater where TBMs game metrics (more visits regardless of quality, fewer complaint calls because they 'look bad').

MITIGATION: never report activity metrics in isolation — always paired with outcome metrics. Correlation insights surfaced as coaching prompts to RBMs (not as scorecards to TBMs). Use anonymized cohort benchmarking rather than individual public ranking. Periodic review by L&D to ensure correlations reflect actual best practices rather than artifacts.
relatedUseCases: Field Force #2 - Activity Analytics (Growthbook + SAP)
Field Force #5 - AI-Enabled Sales Coaching
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Build correlation pipeline joining activity metrics (visit count, time allocation, call mix) with outcome metrics (primary/secondary sales, scheme participation, tier movement); surface as coaching-prompt outputs to RBMs; anonymized cohort benchmarking surface to TBMs; periodic L&D review of surfaced patterns.

description: Classification of each TBM-dealer interaction into activity type: sales / scheme / complaint / collection / discovery / training (taxonomy TBD with Sales leadership); derived from LLM extraction of Dealer Voice Capture transcripts + TBM-confirmed labels
notesOpenQuestions: Two-phase: workshop with Sales Head + RBMs + L&D to define taxonomy and decision rules; then LLM-assisted classification of voice transcripts with TBM correction loop for accuracy. Cross-UC: feeds UC #11 (Sales Coaching — coaching depends on call-type-specific patterns), UC #14 (Churn Early Warning — spike in complaint calls is a churn signal).
relatedUseCases: Field Force #2 - Activity Analytics (Growthbook + SAP)
Field Force #5 - AI-Enabled Sales Coaching
Field Force #8 - Early Warning for Sales Performance & Dealer Churn Risk
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Phase 1 (Definition): workshop with Sales Head + RBMs + L&D to define call-type taxonomy and decision rules. Phase 2 (Labeling): LLM classifier trained on voice transcripts; TBM correction UI for accuracy validation; iterate until accuracy threshold met. Phase 3 (Governance): periodic taxonomy review as business evolves.

description: Per-visit record capturing dealer code, timestamp in/out (geo-fenced check-in), duration, call type (derived from voice transcript), outcome, action items; bundled multi-field record reconciling Saathi check-in + voice capture metadata
notesOpenQuestions: Partially captured via Saathi check-in today, but coverage / discipline is inconsistent. Mandatory geo-fenced check-in needed for reliable capture. Call type field is derived from voice transcript classification (see Call Type Classification field) and is uncaptured today.
relatedUseCases: Field Force #2 - Activity Analytics (Growthbook + SAP)
Field Force #5 - AI-Enabled Sales Coaching
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Standardize Saathi check-in workflow (geo-fenced, mandatory before voice capture); merge with voice-capture metadata; LLM-derive call type from transcript; surface as activity event stream feeding visit-count and time-allocation analytics.

description: Applies discount slab logic from scheme definition to a quantity (hypothetical query or actual transaction); returns expected discount %, absolute discount value, claim amount, slab band. Surfaces 'what's my best deal' optimization across stacked schemes (if stacking is permitted).
notesOpenQuestions: Calculation complexity scales with scheme structure: simple flat % discount is easy; stacked schemes with slab dependencies and exclusion rules are non-trivial. Field complexity depends on stacking rules answer from discovery.
relatedUseCases: Field Force #3 - Pricing, Policy & Scheme Dissemination Chatbot
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Slab-logic engine driven by Scheme Definition rules; stacking-rule handler (additive / multiplicative / mutually exclusive); claim value projection for the queried quantity; surfaces calculation breakdown for transparency.

description: Audit log of every chatbot interaction: text/voice query, user (TBM ID or Dealer Code), language, timestamp, chatbot answer, confidence score, cited circular passages, user feedback (helpful / not helpful / escalated to Commercial Team). Feeds the learning loop for circular ambiguity detection and high-frequency question surfacing.
notesOpenQuestions:
STRATEGICALLY VALUABLE — the query log is the only place where the operational ground truth of 'what's actually being asked about schemes' lives. Surfaces (a) circular ambiguities (low-confidence query clusters); (b) high-frequency questions worth proactive content; (c) GAPS BETWEEN FORMAL DOCUMENTATION AND OPERATIONAL REALITY — audit-worthy finding for HO Leadership.

Voice queries: RECOMMENDED v2 not v1 (text accuracy + RAG must be proven first before adding STT errors on top of LLM errors).
relatedUseCases: Field Force #3 - Pricing, Policy & Scheme Dissemination Chatbot
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Build chatbot UI in Saathi App (text input for v1, voice for v2); log every Q&A pair with full context (user, dealer being asked about, language, confidence, citations); feedback collection UI (thumbs up/down, escalate option); persistent storage with PII handling protocol.

description: Per-answer chatbot confidence score (0–1); below threshold triggers escalation pattern ('I'm not certain about this — please contact your Commercial Team representative'). Conservative threshold critical because scheme errors have material financial impact (claim rejections, dealer trust damage).
notesOpenQuestions:
CRITICAL RISK-MANAGEMENT FIELD. Conservative threshold + escalation behavior is non-negotiable for go-live. The chatbot should never confidently hallucinate scheme rules — hallucinated confidence is more dangerous than uncertainty.

Design principle: prefer 'I don't know, escalate' over wrong answers. Threshold calibration during pilot to balance escalation rate (too many escalations = chatbot useless) vs. error rate (too many errors = chatbot dangerous).
relatedUseCases: Field Force #3 - Pricing, Policy & Scheme Dissemination Chatbot
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Confidence scoring from RAG retrieval quality (citation match score) + LLM self-reported confidence + structural-completeness check (did the chatbot find all relevant scheme criteria for the dealer query?); threshold calibration during pilot; escalation routing to Commercial Team contact.

description: Raw scheme circular PDF as issued by HO (or regional authority); the legal source of truth for scheme terms. Metadata captured alongside: BU, issuing authority, scope (national / regional / BU-specific / tier-specific / SKU-specific), validity start/end, supersession (which prior circulars are replaced), version, issue date.
notesOpenQuestions:
RELATIONSHIP TO SCHEME DEFINITION (UC #7): Scheme Circular Document is the raw legal artifact; Scheme Definition is the structured extract. The chatbot's RAG cites back to specific circular passages; structured rules drive eligibility and discount computation. Keep both fields — they serve different purposes.

DISCOVERY OPEN: where circulars live today (central SharePoint, BU folders, WhatsApp, individual PCs); ingestion approach (push by Commercial Team on publish vs. scheduled pull); format consistency across Seeds / CP / SPN; versioning practice (amendments vs. re-issuance).

SCOPE METADATA is critical for chatbot filtering — not all circulars apply to all dealers. Scope filtering is a hard requirement before chatbot can answer eligibility queries reliably.
relatedUseCases: Field Force #3 - Pricing, Policy & Scheme Dissemination Chatbot
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Phase 1: identify central repository for all circulars (or build one if fragmented); establish ingestion process (push by Commercial Team on publish, with metadata required at upload). Phase 2: LLM extraction pipeline parses circulars into structured Scheme Definition records; metadata fields (scope, validity, supersession) captured manually or LLM-extracted with human validation. Phase 3: versioning + supersession workflow.

description: Frequency analysis of query types over rolling periods; identifies (a) high-volume FAQ candidates for proactive content surfaces; (b) high-question schemes that may indicate over-complexity needing scheme-design simplification.
notesOpenQuestions: Questions that shouldn't need asking are themselves signal — a scheme generating 500 monthly eligibility queries is too complex relative to its design intent. Feeds scheme-design improvement loop to Commercial Team.
relatedUseCases: Field Force #3 - Pricing, Policy & Scheme Dissemination Chatbot
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Aggregation pipeline on Chatbot Query Log (query topic classification + frequency over rolling windows); dashboard surface for Commercial Team / Product Marketing; trigger for proactive FAQ content generation.

description: Periodic analysis of low-confidence query patterns surfaces specific circular clauses that are ambiguous and need clarification in next revision. Also detects gaps between formal documentation (circulars) and operational reality (what dealers actually ask) — surfaces undocumented practices that need governance attention.
notesOpenQuestions:
AUDIT-WORTHY OUTPUT — not just tactical content improvement. Surfaces (a) ambiguous circular language causing repeated low-confidence queries; (b) operational practices that aren't documented anywhere formal (spot schemes, informal commitments, dealer-specific terms); (c) governance gaps where ground-level practice has drifted from HO intent.

Feeds Commercial Team's scheme design + HO Leadership's audit cycle.
relatedUseCases: Field Force #3 - Pricing, Policy & Scheme Dissemination Chatbot
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Cluster analysis on Chatbot Query Log (low-confidence queries, escalations, queries with conflicting answers across users); map clusters back to specific circular passages; periodic report to Commercial Team + HO Leadership.

description: Real-time evaluation per query: combines Scheme Definition rules + Dealer attributes (tier, region, BU, historical participation, YTD purchase) → returns eligible / not eligible / partial / conditional. Surfaced to chatbot user with explanation of which criteria are met / unmet.
notesOpenQuestions: Eligibility logic depends on scheme stacking rules (discovery open). Recommend: surface result with explicit reasoning chain (e.g., 'Eligible because: Gold tier ✓, North region ✓, 250+ units YTD ✓'). Improves transparency and reduces dispute risk.
relatedUseCases: Field Force #3 - Pricing, Policy & Scheme Dissemination Chatbot
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Rules engine evaluating Scheme Definition criteria against live dealer context (real-time SAP + Growthbook lookups); reasoning chain output for transparency; conflict resolution logic for overlapping schemes (governance question, not technical).

description: Per-TBM per-dimension score derived from LLM analysis of voice captures against the Conversation Quality Framework rubrics. Surfaces strengths (high scores) and gaps (low scores). Surfaced to RBM with cohort-anonymized peer comparison; raw scores not shown to TBM directly — RBM contextualizes.
notesOpenQuestions:
Score accuracy depends on (a) framework rubric quality, (b) LLM rating reliability across Indian-language captures, (c) sufficient capture volume per TBM for statistical stability. Recommend showing only rolling N-capture averages, not individual capture scores, to dampen noise.

PRIVACY: scores are RBM-visible; TBMs see only their own dimension-level trends (no peer comparison); HO sees only anonymized cohort distributions.
relatedUseCases: Field Force #5 - AI-Enabled Sales Coaching
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: LLM scoring pipeline: each voice capture transcript rated against framework rubrics, scores per dimension; aggregated to rolling TBM-level averages; calibration loop with human raters (RBMs / L&D) to ensure LLM scores correlate with expert judgement.

description: LLM-generated specific coaching prompts grounded in (a) actual voice excerpts from the TBM's recent captures, (b) activity patterns, (c) peer benchmark gaps. Format: 'In your last 12 Platinum visits, you spent <5 min on cross-sell. Top-decile peers spend 12+ min. Here's an excerpt from your visit on [date]: [quote]. Consider asking: [example questions].' Routed to RBM, not directly to TBM.
notesOpenQuestions:
DESIGN PRINCIPLE: AI surfaces patterns + suggests prompts; RBM delivers feedback to TBM as a human coach. Avoids 'TBMs being graded by a machine' failure mode.

Grounding in actual voice excerpts is critical — generic coaching advice is what already exists. Specificity is what makes this useful.

Frequency: recommend weekly batch per TBM for RBMs to review, plus on-demand pull when RBM opens TBM coaching view.
relatedUseCases: Field Force #5 - AI-Enabled Sales Coaching
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: LLM coaching-recommendation pipeline: identify TBM's weakest dimensions vs. cohort; pull specific voice excerpts illustrating gap; generate concrete suggested coaching prompts grounded in excerpts; RBM review surface in Saathi App or coaching dashboard.

description: Per-coaching-cycle action items captured by RBM after coaching conversation (e.g., 'try discovery questions on next 5 Platinum visits', 'use Should-Cost framing on price-objection calls'). Completion status detected from subsequent voice captures (LLM checks whether TBM actually attempted the technique) or RBM confirmation in next 1:1. Closes the coaching feedback loop.
notesOpenQuestions:
CLOSES THE LEARNING LOOP. Without action item tracking, coaching becomes one-shot — RBM gives feedback, no one knows if it landed. With tracking, coaching becomes iterative.

Detection via voice captures requires LLM to check 'did the TBM use technique X in this conversation?' which depends on rubric clarity from Conversation Quality Framework.

Cross-UC: feeds back into TBM Conversation Quality Score over time (improvement signal).
relatedUseCases: Field Force #5 - AI-Enabled Sales Coaching
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: RBM UI for action item capture during coaching session (text or voice); LLM detection on subsequent voice captures (did the TBM attempt the technique?); RBM confirmation surface in next 1:1; tracking dashboard showing action-item completion rates per TBM and coaching effectiveness over time.

description: TBM's metrics positioned against anonymized peer cohort matched by region, BU, and tenure. Surfaces percentile or band ('30th percentile on cross-sell time') rather than individual public ranking ('behind TBM X'). Designed to motivate growth without triggering competitive resentment.
notesOpenQuestions:
Cohort definition matters — too narrow (5 peers in same region / BU / tenure) makes anonymization weak; too broad (national cohort) makes comparison less meaningful. Recommend region-level cohorts with minimum N (e.g., 20+ peers) for meaningful anonymization.

Applied across multiple metrics: activity (visit count, time allocation), outcome (sales attainment), conversation quality (framework scores).
relatedUseCases: Field Force #5 - AI-Enabled Sales Coaching
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Cohort matching logic (region + BU + tenure band); percentile / band computation per metric per TBM; anonymization layer (minimum cohort size); RBM-facing visualization.

description: Foundational coaching taxonomy: defines coachable dimensions of TBM-dealer conversations — e.g., discovery quality, cross-sell attempts, objection-handling pattern, closing technique, scheme-explanation accuracy, empathy / relationship signals. Includes scoring rubrics and example markers per dimension. Workshop-driven definition with L&D + RBMs + Sales Head.
notesOpenQuestions:
FOUNDATIONAL ASSET for this UC. Quality of coaching depends entirely on whether the framework captures what good selling actually looks like at SFS — not generic 'consultative selling' literature but SFS-specific patterns (e.g., what does great Kharif-season cross-sell look like? what does empathy look like with a Bronze dealer facing inventory pressure?).

Dimensions and rubrics should evolve as the cohort matures — plan for v1 with 5–7 dimensions, refined annually.
relatedUseCases: Field Force #5 - AI-Enabled Sales Coaching
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Phase 1: workshop series with L&D + senior RBMs + Sales Head to define dimensions, rubrics, marker examples (3–4 weeks). Use top-performing TBMs' voice captures as exemplars. Phase 2: encode rubrics in LLM evaluation prompts; validate against held-out captures with human raters. Phase 3: governance — framework version control, periodic review with L&D.

description: Per-dealer total SFS revenue aggregated across Seeds + CP + SPN over rolling periods, with BU-mix breakdown. The core rollup that everything else in UC #12 depends on. Requires cross-BU dealer code reconciliation (UC #10 prerequisite) to aggregate correctly.
notesOpenQuestions:
HARD DEPENDENCY: requires accurate Dealer BU Presence (UC #10) and resolved cross-BU dealer code reconciliation. If dealer codes are split across BUs without a master mapping, this aggregation will under-count or double-count.

Refresh cadence: monthly is likely sufficient for HO Leadership consumption; not a real-time use case.
relatedUseCases: Field Force #6 - Unified Product & Partner-Level Visibility
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Resolve cross-BU dealer code reconciliation (prerequisite); build aggregation pipeline joining Primary Sales (and optionally Secondary Sales) per BU → dealer → period; surface dashboard with BU-mix breakdown and trend over time.

description: Post-launch tracking of revenue change at affected dealers vs. matched control cohort; surfaces (a) incremental growth (launch grew dealer's total SFS wallet), (b) cannibalization (launch shifted dollars from another SFS BU at same dealer), (c) net impact on cross-BU portfolio. Avoids over-interpretation by surfacing patterns descriptively rather than asserting causation.
notesOpenQuestions:
Attribution methodology: control-group matching (dealers with similar profile not exposed to launch) is the cleanest approach, but small sample sizes and confounds (regional market dynamics, competitive activity) make causal claims unsafe.

RECOMMENDATION: surface patterns descriptively ('Dealer X's CP grew 30% and SPN dropped 25% post-launch') without asserting cannibalization causally. Let Product Marketing + BHs interpret with full context.
relatedUseCases: Field Force #6 - Unified Product & Partner-Level Visibility
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Define product launch event registry (which SKUs launched when, which dealers exposed); build control-cohort matching logic; pre/post comparison pipeline with explicit uncertainty bounds; descriptive surfacing to Product Marketing + BHs.

description: Unified cross-BU product taxonomy: BU → category → sub-category → SKU mapping that lets cross-BU rollups, cross-sell analysis, and cannibalization detection work consistently across Seeds / CP / SPN. Likely doesn't exist today as a unified taxonomy — each BU has its own.
notesOpenQuestions:
FOUNDATIONAL ASSET. Similar in scope and approach to UC #1's Spend Category Taxonomy — workshop-driven definition with BU heads + Product Marketing + Sales Ops, then LLM-assisted labeling of historical material master.

Key design question: should there be a single unified hierarchy across BUs, or three BU-specific hierarchies with a thin cross-BU rollup layer on top? Likely the latter is more realistic given category teams within each BU.
relatedUseCases: Field Force #6 - Unified Product & Partner-Level Visibility
Enterprise Assistant #1 - Enterprise Chat Assistant (Supply & Sales)
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Phase 1 (Definition): workshop with BU heads (Seeds / CP / SPN) + Product Marketing + Sales Ops to define hierarchy structure — single unified vs. BU-specific with rollup layer. Phase 2 (Application): LLM-assisted labeling of SAP material master against hierarchy; BU teams validate. Phase 3 (Governance): version control + new-SKU governance for ongoing additions.

description: For each dealer × BU they don't currently carry, computed opportunity score combining: (a) rules-based signals (dealer has adjacent BUs, sufficient scale, geography supports it), (b) pattern-based signals (similar dealers in similar territories carry this BU successfully). Surfaced to Sales Head + RBMs for proactive cross-sell campaigns.
notesOpenQuestions:
Two-layer scoring approach:
- Rules layer: dealer has Seeds + CP, missing SPN, sufficient scale + adjacent geography → opportunity
- Pattern layer: 'dealers like this dealer' similarity analysis — dealers with matching tier / region / BU mix that successfully carry the missing BU

Granularity question: score at BU level (dealer missing entire SPN) or product-category level (dealer has SPN but not micronutrients)? Recommend BU level for v1, refine later.
relatedUseCases: Field Force #6 - Unified Product & Partner-Level Visibility
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Build two-layer scoring pipeline: rules engine for BU-presence gaps + adjacency signals; pattern-similarity model (clustering on tier / region / BU mix / sales pattern); surface ranked opportunities per RBM territory.

description: Unique identifier for each SFS-owned warehouse / plant; primary key for stock aggregation, allocation, and dispatch decisions
relatedUseCases: Field Force #7 - Real-Time Warehouse Stock Visibility

description: Stock dispatched from a warehouse but not yet received at destination (in flight); currently visible in fragments across SAP + Logistics System but not consolidated. Bundled multi-field: SKU, quantity, source warehouse, destination, dispatch date, expected arrival, carrier / vehicle.
notesOpenQuestions:
CSV: 'In-transit data from Logistics system / SAP'. Critical for accurate TBM commitments — a TBM seeing 'warehouse X has 0 units' may miss that 50 units are in transit and will arrive in 2 days.

Consolidation requires joining SAP dispatch records + Logistics System tracking. Likely a derived view rather than a single-source field.
relatedUseCases: Field Force #7 - Real-Time Warehouse Stock Visibility
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Build consolidation layer joining SAP dispatch records + Logistics System tracking; surface in-transit stock alongside warehouse stock in Saathi with expected arrival date; reconcile when stock is received at destination warehouse.

description: Mapping of which SKUs are acceptable substitutes for each other; supports 'if primary SKU out of stock, suggest substitute' workflow in Saathi at order entry. Net-new asset requiring workshop-driven definition with Product Marketing + Sales.
notesOpenQuestions:
Doesn't exist today as structured master data. Today substitution is rule-of-thumb / TBM judgement.

Definition complexity: substitution rules likely have nuance — 'SKU A can substitute for SKU B in geography X but not Y due to crop variety differences'; 'larger pack size of same molecule is an acceptable substitute'. Workshop with Product Marketing + Sales + technical / agronomy teams.
relatedUseCases: Field Force #7 - Real-Time Warehouse Stock Visibility
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Workshop with Product Marketing + Sales + technical teams to define substitution rules per SKU; encode as master data; governance for ongoing additions as new SKUs launch; integrate into Saathi stock view for substitution suggestions.

description: Real-time stock-on-hand per SKU per SFS-owned warehouse; the core inventory data point surfaced to TBMs at order entry
notesOpenQuestions: REFRESH CADENCE TRADE-OFF: Real-time (each Saathi query hits SAP directly) gives accuracy but may strain SAP performance at scale (hundreds of TBMs, multiple queries/day). Near-real-time (5-minute snapshots cached in Saathi data layer) is lighter on SAP but creates stale-data risk. Confirm preferred approach with SFS IT during integration design; pair with Staleness Indicator field for UX transparency either way.
relatedUseCases: Field Force #7 - Real-Time Warehouse Stock Visibility
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Build SAP → Saathi integration layer; decide refresh cadence (real-time API vs. cached snapshot); load testing for SAP performance at TBM-scale concurrent queries; surface staleness indicator alongside stock value.

fieldName: Stock Batch / Expiry Date (per SKU × Warehouse × Batch)
description: Batch-level stock records with expiry dates per SKU per warehouse; basis for FEFO (first-expiry-first-out) allocation and expiry-risk dashboard. Bundled multi-field: batch number, manufacture date, expiry date, quantity in batch.
notesOpenQuestions:
HIGHEST-VALUE FIELD for this UC. Without batch-level expiry tracking, expiry-risk dashboard and FEFO allocation cannot function.

DISCOVERY OPEN: Seeds and CP are typically batch-tracked (regulatory requirement for agri-inputs in India). SPN coverage may be inconsistent. Audit needed: what % of SKUs across BUs have batch + expiry capture in SAP?
relatedUseCases: Field Force #7 - Real-Time Warehouse Stock Visibility
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Audit batch / expiry capture coverage per BU; standardize batch capture process for any gap SKUs; integrate batch-level data into Saathi stock view; build expiry-buffer rules per SKU category (Seeds vs. CP vs. SPN).

description: Stock-on-hand per SKU at vendor / external warehouse locations (3PL, contract manufacturers, distribution partners holding SFS inventory); bundled multi-field: vendor location, SKU, quantity, last-updated timestamp, data source (API / CSV / Excel)
notesOpenQuestions:
CSV: 'currently manually updated in SAP by HO' — meaning stale, error-prone, periodic. Target: API integration with vendor systems where vendors support it; scheduled CSV / Excel ingestion otherwise. Phased rollout based on vendor feasibility + acceptance.

PRIORITY: top 5–10 highest-volume vendor warehouses first. Vendor acceptance to integrate is a real dependency; some vendors may refuse API access citing their own system limitations or competitive concerns.

STALENESS UX: must be surfaced clearly — freshness varies across vendor sources (live API feed vs. weekly CSV upload).
relatedUseCases: Field Force #7 - Real-Time Warehouse Stock Visibility
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Phase 1: identify top vendor warehouses by SFS stock volume; assess each vendor's system + willingness to integrate. Phase 2: API integration for vendors with capable systems; scheduled CSV / Excel ingestion (e.g., daily upload via vendor portal) for others. Phase 3: surface vendor stock alongside SFS stock in Saathi with explicit source + freshness labels.

description: Latitude and longitude coordinates for each warehouse; used for distance-to-dealer routing and recommended source warehouse logic
notesOpenQuestions: Likely captured for major warehouses but may be missing or imprecise for smaller facilities. Audit needed during pilot.
relatedUseCases: Field Force #7 - Real-Time Warehouse Stock Visibility
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Audit warehouse master for geo-coordinate coverage; back-capture missing coordinates.

description: Optimization output recommending which warehouse should fulfill a given dealer order, combining: dealer location, multi-source stock availability (SFS + vendor + in-transit), expiry status (FEFO), and cost-to-serve (geography). Surfaced to TBM at order entry with rationale and alternatives.
notesOpenQuestions: OPTIMIZATION OBJECTIVE OPEN: Lowest cost-to-serve (pure geography), FEFO (expiry-driven), or hybrid? Likely hybrid with category-specific weighting (Seeds = FEFO-heavy due to seasonal expiry; SPN = geography-heavy due to lower expiry pressure). Workshop with Dispatch + Logistics + Supply Chain Head.
relatedUseCases: Field Force #7 - Real-Time Warehouse Stock Visibility
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Build optimization logic: input dealer geo + order SKU × quantity → enumerate warehouses with stock → apply scoring (geography + expiry + cost) → return ranked recommendation with rationale; allow TBM override with audit trail.

description: Boolean flag triggered when stock-on-hand per SKU at a warehouse drops below threshold; routes to Dispatch Planner + RBM for replenishment action; supports pre-emptive 'your dealers typically buy this SKU — stock is low' alerts to relevant TBMs.
notesOpenQuestions: Threshold definition: fixed per SKU × warehouse vs. seasonality-adjusted (Kharif demand spike means earlier alerts for relevant SKUs). Recommend fixed cutoffs for v1; iterate to adaptive after baseline operational data exists.
relatedUseCases: Field Force #7 - Real-Time Warehouse Stock Visibility
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Define threshold rules per SKU × warehouse (workshop with Dispatch + Sales Ops); build alerting engine; route alerts to Dispatch Planner + relevant RBM via dashboard + Saathi push; pre-emptive TBM alerts based on Saathi dealer-purchase-pattern history.

description: Aggregated view of stock approaching expiry (e.g., <90 days remaining for Seeds, <60 days for CP, category-specific buffers); feeds Commercial Team for proactive liquidation scheme design (links to UC #9 Scheme Chatbot). Surfaces SKU × warehouse × quantity × days-to-expiry combinations needing action.
notesOpenQuestions:
Category-specific expiry buffers required (Seeds vs. CP vs. SPN shelf-life norms differ materially). Workshop with Product Marketing + Commercial to define thresholds.

Cross-UC link: feeds UC #9 — Commercial Team uses this view to design liquidation schemes pushed via scheme chatbot to dealers.
relatedUseCases: Field Force #7 - Real-Time Warehouse Stock Visibility
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Define expiry buffers per SKU category; aggregate stock by SKU × warehouse × days-to-expiry; surface dashboard for Commercial Team with severity bands; link to scheme design workflow for liquidation campaigns.

description: Per-stock-record metadata flag indicating data freshness: 'Live' (real-time SAP query), 'Updated N minutes ago' (cached snapshot), 'Updated N hours / days ago' (vendor CSV / Excel feed), 'Stale — verify before committing' (freshness exceeds threshold). Critical UX element so TBMs don't over-commit on stale data, especially for vendor warehouse stock with variable refresh cadence.
notesOpenQuestions:
CSV explicitly mentions 'staleness indicators' (line 438). Foundational UX field — the value of vendor warehouse integration is undermined if TBMs can't tell what's fresh and what isn't.

Thresholds per source: SFS-owned SAP feed = live or <5 min stale; vendor API = <30 min; vendor CSV / Excel = up to 24 hours. Tune during pilot.
relatedUseCases: Field Force #7 - Real-Time Warehouse Stock Visibility
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Metadata layer tracking last-update timestamp per stock record per source; thresholds defining 'Live / Recent / Stale' per source type; UI surface in Saathi (color-coded badge or label alongside stock quantity) so TBMs see freshness at a glance.

description: Per-dealer rolling-window trend in order frequency + volume (3/6/12-month windows); surfaces directional change beyond point-in-time low values. A dealer with stable low volume is different from a dealer in rapid decline — trajectory captures the difference.
notesOpenQuestions:
Trajectory is the dominant input to the churn risk score. Steep declines warrant immediate intervention; gradual declines warrant coaching-level attention.

Seasonality adjustment: Kharif / Rabi cycles mean order patterns are not flat year-round; trajectory should be measured against same-period prior years where possible.
relatedUseCases: Field Force #8 - Early Warning for Sales Performance & Dealer Churn Risk
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Build rolling-window aggregation on Primary Sales per dealer; seasonality adjustment vs. prior-year same-period; trajectory classification (stable / declining / steep decline / churned); refresh monthly.

description: Per-risk-event intervention record: triggered date, TBM action taken, RBM escalation if any, Central Finance involvement if any, outcome (recovered / improved / continued decline / churned), captured-via-voice notes. Closes the learning loop — feeds back into model effectiveness measurement.
notesOpenQuestions:
CLOSES THE LEARNING LOOP. Without intervention tracking, the churn model can't learn whether interventions actually work, and the RBM / Sales Head can't assess intervention playbook effectiveness.

REUSES SAATHI VOICE INFRASTRUCTURE (UC #10): TBM records 'I called Dealer X, he's frustrated about Q3 scheme payout delay, agreed to place a small order next week if we expedite the payout'. LLM extracts: intervention type, dealer commitments, blockers, urgency markers, outcome signals.

Feeds back into next-cycle Dealer Churn Risk Score — successful intervention reduces score; failed intervention escalates severity.
relatedUseCases: Field Force #8 - Early Warning for Sales Performance & Dealer Churn Risk
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Saathi UI for intervention capture (voice + light structured fields: dealer, factor addressed, planned action); LLM extraction of intervention type + dealer commitments + blockers from voice transcripts; outcome tracking via subsequent sales / engagement / voice signals; effectiveness dashboard for RBM + Sales Head + Central Finance.

description: Dealer's GST registration status pulled from GST Portal via GSP: Active / Cancelled / Suspended / Provisional. Inactive dealer GSTIN is a high-severity churn signal (and credit hold trigger). Refreshed monthly with daily delta updates.
notesOpenQuestions:
DEALER-SPECIFIC FIELD. Parallel vendor-specific field exists separately (GSTIN Registration Status — UC #5). The underlying GSP integration / pipeline is shared between vendor and dealer use cases (single contract, single API), but the fields are entity-specific because vendor and dealer GSTINs are distinct populations with different operational implications.

Dealer GST status going inactive routes to Central Finance + Sales (not Procurement / Risk as for vendors).
relatedUseCases: Field Force #8 - Early Warning for Sales Performance & Dealer Churn Risk
thirdPartySourceName: GST Portal via GSP (shared pipeline with UC #5)
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Extend UC #5's GSP integration to ingest dealer GSTIN status (already-contracted single pipeline); build dealer-specific alert routing (Central Finance + Sales); monthly refresh + daily delta updates.

description: Per-dealer breakdown of which factors are driving the churn risk score and by how much; supports the CSV's 'contributing-factor breakdown' requirement. Surfaces 'order frequency dropped 40% (contributing 25 pts), three complaint calls in voice captures (contributing 18 pts), GST filing missed (contributing 15 pts), scheme non-participation in last 2 cycles (contributing 12 pts)'. Routes specific intervention guidance per factor.
notesOpenQuestions:
Transparency is critical for intervention effectiveness — TBM needs to know WHY a dealer is flagged in order to act appropriately. A complaint-driven flag warrants a different intervention than a GST-default flag.

Factor-specific intervention playbooks: voice-complaint signals → TBM dialogue; GST default → Central Finance escalation; sales decline → RBM coaching review; scheme non-participation → commercial team scheme-fit review.
relatedUseCases: Field Force #8 - Early Warning for Sales Performance & Dealer Churn Risk
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Compute per-factor contribution to risk score; surface ranked factors with intervention guidance per factor; route to TBM dashboard + RBM coaching view + Central Finance credit-hold queue.

description: Boolean flag indicating dealer has missed N consecutive GST returns (threshold TBD with Finance / Compliance). Strong churn risk signal; also a Central Finance credit hold trigger. Routes to Central Finance + Sales (distinct from UC #5's vendor-side routing).
notesOpenQuestions:
DEALER-SPECIFIC FIELD. Parallel vendor-specific field exists separately (GST Default Flag — UC #5). The underlying GSP integration / pipeline is shared (single contract, single API), but the fields are entity-specific due to materially different operational implications: vendor default = ITC loss risk (Procurement / Risk); dealer default = receivables / credit hold concern (Central Finance / Sales).

Threshold for default declaration to be agreed with Finance / Compliance (likely 2 consecutive months missed). Distinguish filed-late from not-filed.
relatedUseCases: Field Force #8 - Early Warning for Sales Performance & Dealer Churn Risk
thirdPartySourceName: GST Portal via GSP (shared pipeline with UC #5)
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Reuse UC #5's GSP filing-history pipeline (already-contracted); apply default rule to dealer GSTINs; build dealer-specific alert routing (Central Finance + Sales); workflow for credit hold trigger at higher-severity thresholds.

description: Weighted composite churn risk score (0–100) per dealer combining: sales decline trajectory, scheme participation drop, complaint call frequency, payment behavior, GST default signals, voice sentiment shifts. Refreshed monthly. Tier-specific weighting (Platinum churn weighted differently than Bronze). Threshold crossing triggers intervention workflow.
notesOpenQuestions:
SCORING INFRASTRUCTURE PARALLEL to UC #5's Composite Vendor Risk Score (page 362c3907-070c-81df-bf42-ced939198c43). Same rules-engine pattern; different inputs (internal sales + engagement signals dominant here vs. external regulatory signals dominant for vendors); different outputs (churn intervention vs. vendor block).

DESIGN: Rules-based weighted scoring for v1 (explainable to RBMs + Sales Head); ML predictive model for v2 once sufficient historical labeled churn data exists.

CROSS-UC VALUE: feeds intervention workflow + Central Finance credit decisions + RBM coaching prioritization (UC #11).
relatedUseCases: Field Force #8 - Early Warning for Sales Performance & Dealer Churn Risk
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Define churn definition + multi-criteria weighting framework with Sales Head + Central Finance (workshop); build scoring pipeline reusing UC #5's rules-engine pattern; integrate signal sources (sales, schemes, voice-derived complaints + sentiment, GST flags); tier-specific weighting; monthly refresh with immediate alerts for high-severity signals.

description: Curated dictionary of every leadership-review metric: definition, formula, source system(s), join semantics, owner / steward, version, validity period. The foundational asset that prevents KPI definition drift across the org. Without it, the chat surface is just a SQL frontend that hallucinates inconsistent answers depending on which team's definition wins.
notesOpenQuestions:
FOUNDATIONAL ASSET — the most strategically valuable single field across all 21 UCs. Without it the chat surface cannot work reliably. Workshop investment is substantial: 6–10 weeks for top 50–100 metrics with Finance + Sales + BHs; ongoing governance to prevent re-drift.

Similar in scope to UC #1's Spend Category Taxonomy + UC #12's Product Hierarchy + UC #11's Conversation Quality Framework — these are all 'definition' assets where the workshop work is the actual value, not the technical build.

Each metric requires: name, definition (1-line), formula (computable), source system(s), join semantics for cross-system metrics, owner / steward, version, valid-from / valid-to dates, related metrics. Examples: 'Gross Margin = Revenue − COGS, where Revenue = SAP S&D Primary Sales Value (net of returns), COGS = SAP Material Master Standard Cost × Quantity Sold, joined on Material Code + Period'.

VERSIONING: when a definition changes, lock historical snapshots at the prior definition (don't restate history); surface 'this metric definition changed on Date X' alongside time-series views.
relatedUseCases: Enterprise Assistant #1 - Enterprise Chat Assistant (Supply & Sales)
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Phase 1 (Discovery): enumerate top 50–100 leadership-review metrics from historical decks + Finance team Q&A history. Phase 2 (Definition workshops, 6–10 weeks): for each metric, agree definition + formula + sources + owner with Finance + Sales + BHs; resolve cross-team definition conflicts. Phase 3 (Encoding): store as structured master with versioning + audit trail; expose to chat assistant + dashboards. Phase 4 (Governance): metric steward council, change-control process, periodic review to prevent re-drift.

description: Auto-generated weekly digest of top 5 metrics moving against trend; pushed proactively to leadership (BH / CFO / Sales Head). Anomaly scoring on Enterprise Metrics Dictionary's tracked metrics surfaces directional changes leadership should know about before they ask.
notesOpenQuestions:
CSV: 'Weekly what-changed digest pushed to leadership: top 5 metrics moving against trend, with drill-down links'. Proactive surfacing reduces dependence on leadership knowing which questions to ask.

Design: anomaly scoring vs. expected trend (seasonality-adjusted) for each tracked metric in dictionary; rank by deviation magnitude + business significance; surface with drill-down links into assistant for follow-up questions.

Same descriptive-vs-causal caveat as UC #12 — surface 'X moved Y%' without asserting cause; leadership uses assistant for follow-up exploration.
relatedUseCases: Enterprise Assistant #1 - Enterprise Chat Assistant (Supply & Sales)
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Anomaly detection pipeline over Enterprise Metrics Dictionary tracked metrics; seasonality adjustment; significance ranking; weekly batch generation; email + dashboard delivery to leadership with assistant follow-up links.

description: Audit log of every leadership query to the Enterprise Assistant: text query, user, timestamp, assistant answer, cited metric(s) + definition version, confidence score, chart generated, user feedback (helpful / not / escalated). Same audit-log pattern as UC #9's Chatbot Query Log. Feeds intent library refinement + repeated-question detection (candidates for pre-built dashboards).
notesOpenQuestions:
Strategically valuable beyond the assistant itself: surfaces (a) which questions leadership actually asks (vs. which the Finance team assumes they care about), (b) repeated questions worth promoting to pre-built dashboards, (c) low-confidence query patterns indicating metric-dictionary gaps.

Confidence threshold + escalation behavior is critical — same risk-management concern as UC #9 Scheme Chatbot. Below threshold, assistant should say 'I'm not certain — please verify with Finance / Sales Ops' rather than hallucinate.

PRIVACY: leadership queries can be sensitive (commercial decisions, individual performance reviews). Access to the query log restricted to assistant owners + leadership themselves.
relatedUseCases: Enterprise Assistant #1 - Enterprise Chat Assistant (Supply & Sales)
whatsNeededToMakeAvailable: Build assistant chat UI with persistent logging; capture full Q&A with metric citations + confidence + chart artifacts; feedback collection UI; periodic analysis pipeline for intent library refinement + dashboard candidate detection.

Cross-UC relevance: enables financial-metric queries (gross margin, collections velocity, working capital) over the metrics dictionary. Also potentially relevant to UC #14 dealer credit risk (receivables ageing per dealer).
